items.title: White House warns staff as Iran bets add to growing insider trading concerns
items.summary: Reuters reported that White House staff were warned against using confidential information after suspicious Iran-linked oil futures bets and fresh scrutiny of prediction markets.

items.summary: Bitget’s IPO Prime will debut with preSPAX, a Republic-issued token tied to SpaceX’s post-IPO performance rather than direct ownership of the company’s shares.

items.title: Institutions' bitcoin positioning lacks conviction; CPI, Iran talks might help
items.summary: Institutions are betting on the bitcoin price hitting $80,000 through call options, but they are also buying downside protection.

items.title: World Liberty signals phased WLFI unlock vote after early holder backlash
items.summary: World Liberty said its WLFI unlock proposal will go through community input before a formal vote, outlining a phased vesting plan rather than a full token release.

items.title: Aethir halts bridge exploit, promises compensation after $90K loss
items.summary: Aethir said it halted a bridge exploit on its Ethereum-linked contracts, limiting losses to under $90,000 after PeckShield estimated $400,000 in damages.

items.title: Covenant AI exits Bittensor over ‘decentralization theatre,’ TAO drops 18%

items.title: XRP adjacent Flare proposes protocol-level MEV capture and 40% inflation cut
items.summary: The proposal would move block building away from individual validators, create a revenue entity called FIRE to buy and burn FLR, and reduce annual token inflation to 3%.

items.title: Japan moves to classify cryptocurrencies as financial products
items.summary: The new rules ban insider trading, require issuers to publish annual disclosures, and impose stricter penalties: up to 10 years in prison and 10 million yen in fines for operating without registration.
